Information commonly applied to all data sets:
- radii of surfaces of truncated cone     (1.1%)

Syst. uncertainty in number of events    (2.5%)
- Low energy monitor events by extrapolation(<1.5%)
A detailed analysis of the Bair and Haas (1973) data
and their comparison with the back-corrected IRMM
cross sections (2014-01-24) showed that the IRMM
16O(n,alpha) data measured with T(p,n) neutrons and
in agreement with the 80% values of Bair and Haas
(1973) had to be upwards (re)normalised by a factor
The data obtained with D(d,n) neutrons below 6 MeV
had to be upwards (re)normalised by a factor of
Both (re)normalisations have been already applied to
the data sets under compilation in EXFOR.
Ref. on 2007NICE Conf. abstract deleted. ERR-9, ERR-S
ERR-10 - corrected according to EXFOR rules.
